name: ai-prompt-input
title: AI Prompt Input
description: Autosizing prompt composer with send/stop states, a tools slot, and caller-authored data restrictions above the field.

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled draft. Omit to let the composer own the draft.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Initial draft for the uncontrolled form.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Fires on every keystroke, in both controlled and uncontrolled forms.

props.name: onSubmit
props.description: Fires on Enter or the send button, with the trimmed draft.

props.name: onStop
props.description: Fires from the stop button while `streaming`.

props.name: streaming
props.description: A response is in flight — send is swapped for stop.

props.name: maxLength
props.description: Enables the character counter, which turns destructive at the limit.

props.name: restrictions
props.description: Caller-authored limits on what may be typed, rendered above the field with a shield icon.

props.name: tools
props.description: Leading slot for attachment, voice, or context controls.

props.name: submitLabel
props.description: Accessible name for the send button.

clinicalNotes: Restrictions are rendered in the composer, immediately above the field, because that is the last moment a clinician can act on them — a generic terms link elsewhere in the product is not disclosure. Write them concretely ("Do not paste identifiers from outside the active encounter"), not as boilerplate. An empty or whitespace-only draft can never be sent, and the stop control is always reachable while a response streams, so a request that is pulling in the wrong context can be cut off rather than waited out.
notes: Renders a form, so Enter-to-send and the submit button share one path. Autosizing comes from the textarea's field-sizing-content, capped at max-h-40.
